data.body:
PASS-1 REVIEW (G6 — approach soundness, anti-patterns & alternative-selection) on "Repo-snapshot isolation for code-reading gates". Grounded live against src/rebar/signing.py:19-25,188-206 and src/rebar/_commands/transition.py:393-396. Confirms and re-affirms the prior G6 seam finding.

AFFIRMATIONS (G6 criteria passed): (1) snapshot materialization mechanism — object-store read, no worktree, atomic dir-rename population + single-flight + delete-on-last-close, read-tree+checkout-index over lossy git archive — logically complete and standard; (2) fitness-for-purpose — attested/local split structurally bars the named footgun (local "NEVER sign"), solving the actual false-negative incident, not a proxy; (3) alternative-selection — object-store materialization is defensibly at-least-as-good vs. `git worktree add` per snapshot on lock-contention/blast-radius; (4) positive rationale present (grounded in Gitaly/Sourcegraph/GitHub-tarball/Bazel/ccache/Nix/in-toto).

FINDINGS: both converge on ONE load-bearing seam — how the pinned SHA becomes part of the SIGNED (not merely recorded) attestation. The existing signer (signing.py:19-25,188-201) signs only `{v, algorithm, ticket_id, manifest}` and records git state as audit-metadata-only BY DESIGN; AC2's "bound to verified_at_sha" + "reproducible" contradicts that unless the plan names the binding mechanism. See structured JSON for the two checklist items + suggested manifest-channel fix (thread `verified_at_sha` as a verified-step into the existing `sign_manifest` manifest — zero payload-schema/version change, no prior-signature invalidation).
